summary: Project Agent Workbench rounds from canonical runtime public stage outputs.
rationale: This rollout intentionally crosses runtime public event emission, runtime-control stage output persistence, Workbench BFF projection, and the React stream client so the Workbench thinking process uses durable runtime_public_* summaries instead of raw runtime-event window reconstruction. The schema, projection, and stream invalidation changes must land together to keep the BFF and frontend contract coherent.
line_count_rationale: Both files were already above the production line-count redline. This PR adds small adapter hooks only: runtime public stage emission in the orchestrator and public stage output allowlisting in RuntimeControlStore. New projection logic lives in src/seektalent_ui/agent_workbench_rounds.py instead of expanding those oversized files further.
verification: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_runtime_control_event_contract.py tests/test_runtime_public_event_contract.py tests/test_runtime_multi_source_round_dispatch.py tests/test_runtime_source_lanes.py tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q
uv run ty check src tests tools
deletion_targets: Workbench thinking process reconstruction from bounded raw runtime-event windows
planned source query intents displayed as executed when a source lane is blocked
runtime finalization changes hidden inside natural-language final summary updates
risks: Public stage outputs must reject provider payloads, raw resumes, prompts, cookies, and tokens while preserving recruiter-visible query and round facts.
The BFF projection must keep final runtime state separate from Conversation Agent finalSummary text.
Frontend stream handling must treat runtimeFinalization.changed as a durable snapshot invalidation event.
